retrospective.harder_than_needed: The S2 architect subagent died on an API stream-idle timeout AFTER landing all code (InvitationCard, groups.tsx wiring, PendingInvitations deletion, i18n keys, badge kind, both e2e spec rewrites, i18n parity test) but BEFORE writing result.json and post-impl verification records. The lead completed the story out-of-band: re-ran typecheck (clean) and the 34 targeted unit tests (pass), grep-verified no dangling refs / dead copy / broken testids, and reviewed the full diff (Stage-1). No implementation gap resulted, but a mid-story architect death required lead recovery rather than a clean architect handoff.
retrospective.surprised_by: The crashed architect had already made the two rewritten e2e specs S3-compatible (tap-to-preview asserts URL then goto('/groups/') to decouple from whether the preview view exists yet) and preserved the pending-invitation-row-<id> testid too, beyond the required stable testids — careful work despite the abrupt termination.
